title: zscalerctl introspect output
description: Schema for the JSON document emitted by `zscalerctl introspect`. The document describes the full CLI surface: commands, flags, resource catalog, and exit codes. It is static (config-free, no tenant data) and is the primary machine-readable contract for agents and tooling.

$schema.description: URL of the JSON Schema that validates this document (this file). Floats on the main branch; the introspect_version field carries the contract version.

introspect_version.description: Contract version of the introspect output format (currently "1"). Increment when field semantics change.

cli_version.description: zscalerctl version string (e.g. "v0.60.0"). Empty when produced by IntrospectTree directly; filled in by the introspect command before serialisation.

read_only.description: True when every command in this CLI surface is read-only (no mutations). Currently always true.

commands.description: All commands in the CLI surface, including hidden and deprecated commands, and virtual {product} {resource} {op} entries derived from the resource catalog.

catalog.description: Snapshot of the resource catalog: products, resources, their operations, and projected field names.

exit_codes.description: Exit-code table mapping integer codes to kind, retryability, and description.

commandDoc.description: Description of a single CLI command for agent consumption.

path.description: Space-joined command path without the root program name (e.g. "config init", "zia locations list").

long.description: Extended description shown in the command's own --help page. Omitted when empty.

aliases.description: Alternative names for the command (always present, may be empty array).

hidden.description: Whether the command is hidden from --help listings.

deprecated.description: Deprecation message; omitted when the command is not deprecated.

mutating.description: Whether the command modifies state. Currently always false (CLI is read-only).

args.description: Positional argument policy for this command.

inherited_flags.description: Names of flags inherited from parent commands (without leading "--").

output_fields.description: Projected field names available for --fields / --filter on resource read commands. Omitted for non-resource commands.

flagDoc.description: Description of a single CLI flag (global or local to a command).

shorthand.description: Single-character shorthand (without leading "-"). Omitted when not set.

type.description: Flag value type as reported by pflag (e.g. "string", "bool", "int", "stringArray").

default.description: Default value as a string. Omitted when empty.

required.description: Whether the flag is required. Currently always false; retained for forward compatibility.

enum.description: Allowed values when the flag accepts an enumerated set. Omitted when unconstrained.

argsDoc.description: Positional argument policy for a command.

policy.description: Argument-count policy: none (no args), exact (exactly N), at_least (at least N), range (0–N), arbitrary (any number).

n.description: Expected argument count for "exact" or upper bound for "range". Omitted when zero.

valid_values.description: Allowed argument values (shell completion candidates). Omitted when unconstrained.

catalogDoc.description: Snapshot of the zscalerctl resource catalog.

products.description: Ordered, deduplicated list of product family names (e.g. ["zia", "zpa", "ztw", "zcc", "zidentity"]).

resources.description: All catalog resources with their operations and projected fields.

resourceDoc.description: A single resource entry in the catalog.

product.description: Product family this resource belongs to (e.g. "zia", "zpa").

ops.description: Read operation names supported by this resource (e.g. ["list", "get"]).

fields.description: Projected field names available for --fields / --filter under standard redaction mode.

code.description: Numeric exit code returned by the process.

retryable.description: Whether a retry is reasonable for this exit code (true only for transient failures like live_access_failed).

description.description: Human-readable description of when this exit code is returned.
